dtcs.description: 64, C15A9/66, C15AA/66, C15AB/66: Actuator Malfunction When the VGRS system is normal, the steering actuator conducts current from the steering control ECU to the steering actuator solenoid to release the lock mechanism, enabling motor operation. The steering actuator does not operate under any of the following conditions: - The engine switch is off. - The motor in the actuator is locked by the lock mechanism to prevent rotation (fail-safe function). - The system is being protected from overheating (fail-safe function). If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the steering actuator lock mechanism or a motor rotation angle error, it will store these DTCs. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE STEERING ACTUATOR ASSEMBLY (a) Replace steering actuator assembly    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Actuator/Removal. NOTICE: Before replacing the steering actuator assembly, make sure to use the Techstream Data List to confirm and record the "Elec Angle Position" value    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Data List / Active Test. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: 61 - Actuator Malfunction The steering control ECU drives the actuator while the steering wheel is turned to set the gear ratio. It detects the current flowing into the actuator motor driving circuit. If the steering control ECU detects excessive current, it will store DTC C15A1/61, turn on the master warning light, and stop VGRS operation. HINT When DTC C15A2/62 is output, a short circuit inside the steering actuator or inside the steering control ECU is suspected. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 62, C15A6/62: Actuator Malfunction The steering control ECU drives the actuator based on steering angle sensor signals and vehicle speed signals. If the steering control ECU detects excessive current flowing into the steering actuator, or an internal malfunction, it will turn on the master warning light, store DTC C15A2/62, and stop VGRS operation. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the motor drive circuit, it will turn the master warning light on, store DTC C15A6/62, and stop VGRS operation. HINT Only the actuator can be checked in this procedure. If it is normal, the steering control ECU is faulty. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 63, C15C5/69: Actuator Malfunction The steering actuator drives the internal motor using the current output from the steering control ECU to change the relative angle between the tire angle and steering wheel angle. The rotation angle sensor in the steering actuator detects the motor rotation angle and outputs this information to the steering control ECU. If the steering control ECU detects a malfunction in the rotation angle sensor circuit, it will store DTC C15A3/63. HINT *: A tire angle of 36° and a motor rotation angle of 36° correspond to the steering wheel angle. The amount of change in tire angle and motor rotation angle is from when the mode is changed to test mode. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 31 and C1235/35 are for the front speed sensor RH. -

36: Front Speed Sensor RH Circuit The speed sensor detects wheel speed and sends the appropriate signals to the ECU. These signals are used to control the Multi-terrain ABS, EBD, BA, VSC, A-TRAC, CRAWL and hill-start assist control systems. This speed sensor contains a sensor IC, which consists of 2 MREs (Magnetic Resistance Element). The speed sensor rotor, which consists of 48 sets of N and S poles that are arranged in a circle, is integrated with the inner race of the hub bearing. To detect the rotation direction, the output waves are used to determine the relationship of the pulses that are generated by 2 MREs. Upon receiving this signal, the sensor IC outputs a forward or backward wave to the skid control ECU. HINT -

32 and C1236/36 are for the front speed sensor LH. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform zero point calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE

When DTCs of the speed sensor (C0200/31, C0205/32, C0210/33, C0215/34, C1235/35, C1236/36,

dtcs.description: 66: Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the engine switch is turned on (IG) and the vehicle is driven at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for approximately 5 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If front wheel alignment or steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the negative battery terminal, or if yaw rate and G sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and outputs this DTC to indicate a poor adjustment. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND G SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and G sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT - When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and G sensor is cleared, steering angle sensor zero point will also be cleared. - If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and G sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 35 km/h (22 mph) or more for at least 5 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set, while driving straight ahead. HINT If front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire yaw rate and G sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION  See: Maintenance/Alignment/Service and Repair/Front Wheel Alignment/Adjustment OK --  END

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: ACTIVE HEIGHT CONTROL SUSPENSION: C1711-C1714: Front Height Control Sensor RH Circuit Malfunction The height control sensor changes its resistance value based on changes in vehicle height. The suspension control ECU outputs a fixed voltage of 5 V to the SHB terminal of the height control sensor. If the sensor resistance value changes, the sensor voltage value changes accordingly. The suspension control ECU then receives the voltage value from the sensor to detect the vehicle height change. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR - SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU) NOTICE: When inspecting the wire harnesses between the suspension control ECU and each sensor, it is difficult to determine the malfunctioning area if there is a sensor power supply malfunction. Therefore, disconnect each sensor connector (front RH, front LH, rear RH and rear LH) on the ECU side. (a) Check the front height control sensor RH side: (C1711) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the z43 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (b) Check the front height control sensor LH side: (C1712) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the z44 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (c) Check the rear height control sensor RH side: (C1713) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the N4 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Check the rear height control sensor LH side: (C1714) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the N6 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR (a) Check the front height control sensor: (1) Remove the front height control sensor RH or front height control sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front Side)/Removal. NOTICE: Do not drop the height control sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (2) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (3) Connect the positive (+) end of the batteries to terminal 3 (SHB) of the height control sensor and the negative (-) end of the batteries to terminal 1 (SHG). While slowly moving the sensor link up and down, measure the voltage between terminal 2 (SHFR*1 or SHFL*2) and terminal 1 (SHG). NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of higher than 6 V. HINT *1: for RH *2: for LH (4) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: for RH for LH (b) Check the rear height control sensor. (1) Remove the rear height control sensor RH or rear height control sensor LH    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear Side)/Removal. NOTICE: Do not drop the height control sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (2) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (3) Connect the positive (+) end of the batteries to terminal 1 (SHB) of the height control sensor and the negative (-) end of the batteries to terminal 3 (SHG). While slowly moving the sensor link up and down, measure the voltage between terminal 2 (SHRR*1 or SHRL*2) and terminal 3 (SHG). NOTICE: Do not apply a voltage of higher than 6 V. HINT *1: for RH *2: for LH (4) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: for RH for LH Result E --  REPLACE REAR HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear Side)/Removal D --  REPLACE REAR HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR RH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Rear Side)/Removal C --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR LH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front Side)/Removal B --  REPLACE FRONT HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR RH  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Ride Height Sensor/Service and Repair/Height Control Sensor (for Front Side)/Removal A --  REPLACE SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Suspension/Suspension Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: ACTIVE HEIGHT CONTROL SUSPENSION: C1711-C1714: Front Height Control Sensor RH Circuit Malfunction The height control sensor changes its resistance value based on changes in vehicle height. The suspension control ECU outputs a fixed voltage of 5 V to the SHB terminal of the height control sensor. If the sensor resistance value changes, the sensor voltage value changes accordingly. The suspension control ECU then receives the voltage value from the sensor to detect the vehicle height change. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (HEIGHT CONTROL SENSOR - SUSPENSION CONTROL ECU) NOTICE: When inspecting the wire harnesses between the suspension control ECU and each sensor, it is difficult to determine the malfunctioning area if there is a sensor power supply malfunction. Therefore, disconnect each sensor connector (front RH, front LH, rear RH and rear LH) on the ECU side. (a) Check the front height control sensor RH side: (C1711) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the z43 sensor connector. Standard Resistance: (c) Check the rear height control sensor RH side: (C1713) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the N4 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Check the rear height control sensor LH side: (C1714) (1) Disconnect the K31 and K32 ECU connectors. (2) Disconnect the N6 sensor connector. (3) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: ACTIVE HEIGHT CONTROL SUSPENSION: C1715-C1717, C1796-C1798: Front Acceleration Sensor RH Malfunction The acceleration sensor (up and down G sensor) detects the upward and downward acceleration of the vehicle, and outputs it as a voltage to the suspension control ECU. Up and down G sensors are installed in 3 locations: 1) the suspension control ECU, 2) the driver side instrument panel, and 3) the passenger side instrument panel. Each up and down G sensor independently detects the upward and downward acceleration. During a test mode inspection, the suspension control ECU reads the fluctuations in the signal of each sensor. When the sensor cannot detect +/-1.96 m/s2 for 1 second or more during test mode, DTCs C1796, C1797 and C1798 are stored. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. - If DTCs C1717 and C1798 (Rear Acceleration Sensor Malfunction) are stored, replace the suspension control ECU (houses rear acceleration sensor). PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Front Suspension Control Valve RH Malfunction In order to decrease roll angle/pitch angle, the suspension control ECU turns the spring rate switching valve in the front suspension control valve off/on to change the fluid lines when the acceleration sensor or yaw rate sensor signal is input into the suspension control ECU. Normally, the spring rate switching valve is open. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT Power is supplied to the front suspension control valve and No. 1 height control valve from the suspension control ECU. If a short occurs in the power supply circuit in the No. 1 height control valve, DTC C1736 and/or C1737 will be stored. In this case, DTC C1741, C1742, C1743, C1744, C1753, C1754 and/or C1755 will also be stored. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Abnormal Leveling Valve Oil Pressure The solenoid valve is located in the No. 1 height control valve. When the vehicle height is adjusted, the fluid line is opened or closed according to the signal from the suspension control ECU. If the height control pump and motor activates when the fluid line is closed due to the solenoid valve malfunctioning, the solenoid valve being stuck, or foreign matter clogging the fluid line, fluid pressure increases abnormally. When this problem with the fluid pressure is detected, DTC C1764 and/or DTC C1765 is stored. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: SUSPENSION CONTROL: ACTIVE HEIGHT CONTROL SUSPENSION: C1715-C1717, C1796-C1798: Front Acceleration Sensor RH Malfunction The acceleration sensor (up and down G sensor) detects the upward and downward acceleration of the vehicle, and outputs it as a voltage to the suspension control ECU. Up and down G sensors are installed in 3 locations: 1) the suspension control ECU, 2) the driver side instrument panel, and 3) the passenger side instrument panel. Each up and down G sensor independently detects the upward and downward acceleration. During a test mode inspection, the suspension control ECU reads the fluctuations in the signal of each sensor. When the sensor cannot detect +/-1.96 m/s2 for 1 second or more during test mode, DTCs C1796, C1797 and C1798 are stored. NOTICE: - Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. - If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. - If DTCs C1717 and C1798 (Rear Acceleration Sensor Malfunction) are stored, replace the suspension control ECU (houses rear acceleration sensor). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ACCELERATION SENSOR) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and the Techstream on. INSPECT ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Remove the front acceleration sensor    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Steering and Suspension/Sensors and Switches - Suspension/Acceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: Do not drop the acceleration sensor. If it is dropped, replace it with a new one. (c) Connect 3 1.5 V dry cell batteries in series. (d) Connect the positive (+) end of the batteries to terminal 3 (SGB) of the acceleration sensor and the negative (-) end of the batteries to terminal 2 (SGG). HINT The voltage may differ according to the amount that the sensor is tilted. dtcs.description: 11-C2115/15: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressures to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display tire pressure display screen. (f) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (g) Check that each tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed. Result NOTICE: - It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. - When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270° (including the spare tire). Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. (h) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed, set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value(s). HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has not changed after rechecking, inspect the other problem (malfunction in transmission or reception function)    See: C2121. B --  END A --  GO TO TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER INSPECTION PROCEDURE  See: C2121

dtcs.description: 21-C2125/25, C2181/81-C2185/85: No Signal from Transmitter ID1 in Main Mode The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check for the following conditions described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may turn on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located in or around the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings, or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display tire pressure display screen. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (h) Check the Data List. NOTICE: - It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. - When no "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. - Record the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data transmitter ID that corresponds to each tire. (i) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. Identify the transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. Result C --  END B --  INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU A -- Continue to next step.

HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressures of all tires to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Display the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the Techstream. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure for each tire at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data displayed on the Techstream (ID1 to ID5) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data was the cause of the output DTC. HINT - Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. - Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) corresponds to which tire. (h) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT - *: It may take about 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2125/25    See: C2121. - The tire pressure information reception condition can also be confirmed through the multi-information display tire pressure display screen. Result NOTICE: - It takes about 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. - When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. - Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (i) When an "ID Inflation Pressure" data value (one of ID1 to ID5) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (j) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID5) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. (k) Set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value(s)    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Service Precautions. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. HINT - Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. - The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  END A --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 65-C2169/69: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as tire pressure, and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction and stores DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK TIRES (a) Check that the tires are not flat, and have no indication of tire pressure drop. OK: Tires are normal. HINT If a tire is damaged, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter may also have been damaged at the same time. IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC   See: C2141 NEXT -- Continue to next step.

NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The IDs of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter are registered to the tire pressure warning ECU. When the ECU detects that a transmitter ID code is not registered in the ECU, a DTC is stored. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED ID CODES) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (e) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: The registered transmitter ID codes are displayed on the Techstream screen. NG --  PERFORM REGISTRATION (TRANSMITTER ID) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

PERFORM REGISTRATION (TRANSMITTER ID) (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 76 - Receiver Error This DTC is stored when referencing data becomes impossible due to an internal malfunction in the ECU. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. (d) Clear the DTC    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 77 - Initialization not Completed Initialization is necessary if one of the following occurs: 1) the ECU is replaced; 2) a tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is replaced; 3) tires with a different standard tire pressure are installed; 4) the tires are rotated; or 5) a new vehicle is delivered. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FREQUENCY RECEIVING CONDITION (a) Check for the following conditions described below: (1) Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are located in the vicinity of the vehicle. HINT If the vehicle is located in areas described above, the tire pressure warning light may turn on only in a particular area. (2) Devices using similar radio frequencies are used in the vehicle. OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located in or around the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings, or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 79 - Tire Pressure Monitor ECU Communication Stop The tire pressure warning ECU exchanges speed signals and warning light signals with the combination meter via CAN communication. dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives speed signals from the combination meter via CAN communication. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode, and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is output for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is stored only in test mode. NOTICE: - When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. - It is necessary to perform initialization See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization after registration See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced.
